content: The grant proposal application should include:
Title Page: Include research title, PI and Co-I names and institutions, research authority, application date, and signatures.
Abstracts: Provide English, Hebrew, and Layman's terms abstracts (up to one page each) including background and aim, methods, expected results, importance to medicine, and five keywords.

Research Plan: Maximum 10 pages for proposals, 3 pages for pre-proposals. Include scientific background, research question, study design with a detailed plan, methods (sample size justification and available equipment), preliminary results, schematic representation with partner responsibilities and timeline, and time estimate for each stage. References (up to 15) must be included.

Budget: Describe the budget for the entire research duration according to the electronic form's budget items. Justify the budget request, especially if supported by other grants. Salaries to PI and CoI, permanent equipment, clinics charges, and travels abroad are not eligible costs. Overhead is 10% of the final sum.
Curriculum Vitae: Provide a CV for each applicant with education, employment, and active research grants.
Collaboration Letters: Include letters from collaborators consenting to contribute to the research.
Bio-Ethics Approvals: Include required bio-ethics approvals (if applicable).
Suggested Reviewers: Provide names and emails of at least 5 experts in the field without conflicts of interest.

Checklist: Assemble the proposal in the specified order, including a printed copy of the electronic form, abstracts, research plan, budget, CVs, collaboration letters, bio-ethics approvals, suggested reviewers, and the checklist itself.
cfp_subject: This funding opportunity focuses on supporting medical research projects. It covers various aspects of medical research, including study design, methodology, and ethical considerations, targeting researchers in medicine to improve human health.
